welcome.headline: Pack light.
welcome.deck: Build your gear library, create pack lists, track your weight to the gram — and share with the community.
welcome.step1: Click on things to edit them.
welcome.step2: Click and drag the handle to reorder.
welcome.step3: Use the share link to share your list.
welcome.feature1Text: Add every piece of kit once — weight, brand, category. Reuse across trips, filter, compare.
welcome.feature2Text: Build lists per trip, see base weight / worn / consumables live. Packing mode to check items off as you go.
welcome.feature3Text: Publish lists publicly, follow other hikers, discover curated loadouts from the community.
welcome.textRegister: Save your lists online, sync across devices, and get started in minutes.
welcome.textSignin: Jump back into your lists right away.
welcome.faq1A: ZenPak picks up where LighterPack left off. It keeps the simple public gear-list spirit, then adds a reusable gear library, public profiles, community discovery, packing mode, and active ongoing development.
welcome.faq2A: Yes. The core stays free and open-source, including self-hosting. Kin is a simple support plan for the hosted app, and Wayfarer is the creator layer with affiliate links, promo codes, and insights.
welcome.faq3Q: Can I import my existing lists?
welcome.faq3A: Yes. Export your CSV from lighterpack.com or another spreadsheet-based gear list tool, then import it into ZenPak. Lists, items, weights, and notes come across cleanly, so you can move without rebuilding everything by hand.

list.getStartedStep1: Click on things to edit them. Give your list and category a name.
list.getStartedStep2: Add new categories and give items weights to start the visualization.
list.getStartedStep3: When you're done, share your list with others!

misc.aboutDeck: An open-source gear list tool — built by one person, maintained for the people who care about their base weight.
misc.originP1: Planning my Camino de Santiago, LighterPack was the obvious choice. Everyone in the ultralight community used it. But it hadn't been updated in years — no community features, no profiles, no way for gear creators to share lists with affiliate links without hacking something together.
misc.originP2: So I forked it. Modernized the stack. Built the features I wanted: community discovery, season tags, follow system, creator tools. Kept everything that made LighterPack good — the simplicity, the weight chart, the CSV round-trip.
misc.originP3: No VC, no growth hacking, no gear database trying to tell you what to buy. You manage your gear. The app stays out of the way.
misc.differentItem1: Community discover — browse real lists from real hikers, filter by season and base weight
misc.differentItem2: Season and activity tags — 3-Season, Thru-hike, Winter Camping, Ultralight, on each list
misc.differentItem3: Public profiles and follow system — find the packers worth following
misc.differentItem4: Creator layer — affiliate links and insights, for people who earn from gear recommendations
misc.differentItem5: Self-hosting — fully open-source, no account needed, no lock-in
misc.planDescBase: Free. The full app. No paywalled core features.
misc.planDescKin: Support the project. Enhanced public profile, full JSON export, supporter badge.
misc.planDescWayfarer: Everything in Kin, plus affiliate links, promo codes, and insights (views, copies, clicks).
misc.builtBySuffix: — preparing for the Camino de Santiago, and one day the Shikoku Henro.
misc.kofiCta: put a stone on the cairn
misc.kofiNote: pay what you want on Ko-fi, get 1 year of Kin
misc.notFoundSub: Looks like this path leads nowhere. Maybe the trail was renamed, moved, or never existed.
misc.helpStep1: Click on things to edit them. Give your list and category a name.
misc.helpStep2: Add new categories and items to your list.
misc.helpStep3: When you're done, share your list with others!
misc.helpQtyWornText: If you have multiple quantity of an item and mark that item as worn, only the first quantity will count towards your worn weight. The rest will count towards your pack weight. This is because most items you have multiple of, you only wear one at once. This means you can't list your shoes/trekking poles with weights as individual weights and quantity of two - you should list as the combined weight with quantity of one.
misc.helpLinkedItemsText: If you copy your list or drag an item from the gear library into a second list, those items are now linked. This means that changes to an item in one place will update that list everywhere. If you want to copy your list without links, for now you can export to CSV and re-import the list.
misc.oauthSetupText: Pick a unique username to complete your account setup.
